title: Add report-reader product code and end-to-end validation from VS Code surface to ACP reports
objective: Implement a report-reader surface that can consume .gemini/pcc-reports and expose verdict, artifact path, and runtime metadata to the controller or UI, then validate the path end to end from the VS Code companion surface through ACP-generated reports.
context: Hook scripts already emit structured JSON reports under .gemini/pcc-reports.
No product code currently reads those reports.
Existing UI tests stop at terminal sendText mocks and ACP tests stop before the VS Code surface.
constraints: Reuse the existing report JSON where practical instead of inventing a second ad hoc shape.
Keep UI coupling light in the first pass.
Prefer targeted validation over generic test runners for this area.
required_evidence: A report-reader API or helper in product code.
At least one targeted validation path connecting the UI/controller side to ACP report artifacts.
Proof that verdict and report path can be surfaced without manual file inspection.
success_criteria: Controller or UI code can read PCC reports deterministically.
There is at least one end-to-end or near-end-to-end validation path for report visibility.
The result is suitable for future job dashboards or lane status.
artifacts_expected: Visible controller fields for verdict and report path

next_actions.allow: Implement the report reader and add the targeted validation path.
next_actions.review: Split report reading from UI exposure and ship the smaller part first.
next_actions.reject: Reduce scope to a non-UI report-reader helper and retry.
next_actions.retry: Retry after the controller ledger exists.
